message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In a previous paper we studied the time evolution of the Covid-19 pandemic in Italy during the first wave of 2020 using a number of distribution laws. We concluded that the best distribution law to predict the evolution of the pandemic, if basic conditions of the pandemic (such as distancing measures, use of masks, start of schools, intensive use of public transportation, beginning and end of holidays, vaccination campaign and no significant onset of new Covid variants) do not appreciably change, is a distribution of the type of Planck’s law with three parameters. In our 2020 study we did not use the number of daily positive cases in Italy but the ratio of daily positive cases per number of daily tests, ratio today sometimes referred to as: “
                  <jats:italic>positivity rate</jats:italic>
                  ”. We showed that, if basic conditions do not change, the Planck’s distribution with three parameters provides very good predictions of the
                  <jats:italic>positivity rate</jats:italic>
                  about one month in advance. In particular, in a second paper, using the Planck’s distribution with three parameters, we predicted, about one month in advance, the spread of the pandemic in Italy during the Christmas 2020 holidays with an error of a few percent only. We then study the present (September 2021) evolution of the pandemic in Italy and we show that the Planck’s distribution, based on the data of July and August, predicts well the evolution of the pandemic. In particular, we show that the peak of the
                  <jats:italic>positivity rate</jats:italic>
                  was predicted to occur approximately around the middle of August and that the agreement of this Planck’s function (obtained fitting the data up to 10 July 2021) and the
                  <jats:italic>positivity rate</jats:italic>
                  observed after 5 weeks, on 12 September 2021 is very good. However, the end of the Italian holidays and the start of all the activities including schools, intensive use of public transportation and further changes in distancing measures may cause a discrepancy of the predicted trend of the
                  <jats:italic>positivity rate</jats:italic>
                  of the pandemic with respect to the real observed values.
                </jats:p>
title: Analysis of the prediction of the 2021 time-evolution of the Covid-19 Pandemic in Italy using a Planck’s distribution

reference.article-title: An improved mathematical prediction of the time evolution of the Covid-19 pandemic in Italy, with a Monte Carlo simulation and error analyses

reference.unstructured: The data of the Covid-19 pandemic were obtained from: https://github.com/pcm-dpc/COVID-19/blob/master/dati-andamento-nazionale/dpc-covid19-ita-andamento-nazionale.csv

reference.volume-title: Planck’s Original Papers in Quantum Physics

reference.unstructured: Ignazio Ciufolini and Antonio Paolozzi , Prediction of evolution of the second wave of Covid-19 pandemic in Italy, medRxiv preprint doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.24.20238139
